---
title: High Mining-Concentration Counties
description: Choropleth map showing counties with high mining concentration based
  on ERS typology.
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-map-ers-high-mining/
categories:
  - Chart
  - Geography
  - Rural Development
tags:
  - USDA-ERS
  - County Typology
  - Mining
  - Extractive Industries
  - Map
image: map_ers_high_mining.svg
chart:
  title: High Mining-Concentration Counties
  chartType: map
  interactive: false
  source: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Economic Research Service
  sourceUrl: https://www.ers.usda.gov/data-products/county-typology-codes/
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - usda-county-typology
usesPackages:
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![This map highlights counties classified as mining-dependent by the USDA ERS, where extractive industries play a dominant role in the local economy.](./map_ers_high_mining.svg)

## Overview

This map highlights counties classified as mining-dependent by the USDA ERS, where extractive industries play a dominant role in the local economy.

## Key Findings

- Mining-dependent counties are concentrated in Appalachia, Wyoming, and the Southwest
- These counties face significant transition challenges as energy markets shift
- Mining counties often have boom-bust economic cycles

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/ers_mining_map.R` in the producing project.
